text.title: Engagement Plan and Drift Detection
text.summary: Assessment matrix structure, cadence rules per power/interest quadrant, and drift detection to prevent silent disengagement.

rule.statement: Build the Engagement Assessment Matrix with columns: Stakeholder, Power (H/M/L), Interest (H/M/L), Current Level, Desired Level, Gap (+/-), and Next Activity.
rule.rationale: The matrix makes engagement gaps visible in one view and drives prioritisation — high power + high gap = highest risk to the project.

example.code: | Stakeholder | Power | Interest | Current | Desired | Gap | Next Activity | |----------------|-------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----|-----------------------| | CEO | H | M | Neutral | Supportive | +1 | Demo at Board meeting | | IT Director | H | H | Resistant | Neutral | +1 | 1:1 concern session | | End Users | L | H | Unaware | Supportive | +2 | Training webinar | | Project Sponsor| H | H | Supportive| Leading | +1 | Strategy co-author |

rule.statement: Apply contact cadence by power/interest quadrant: Manage Closely (H/H) weekly; Keep Satisfied (H/L) bi-weekly; Keep Informed (L/H) weekly broadcast; Monitor (L/L) monthly summary.
rule.rationale: Over-communicating to low-interest stakeholders wastes time and trains them to ignore messages. Under-communicating to high-power stakeholders allows silent resistance to grow.

section.title: Drift Detection

rule.statement: Flag a stakeholder as drifting when the last recorded interaction exceeds the cadence threshold for their quadrant with no recorded blocker reason.
rule.rationale: Drift is invisible without a system. A stakeholder who was Supportive three months ago may now be Neutral — and will be classified as Supportive in the risk register until someone checks.

section.reference: Python drift detector by quadrant threshold

antipattern.description: Writing an Engagement Plan that is never executed — no rituals, no cadence calendar.
antipattern.reason: A plan without execution triggers decays to a document nobody reads. Rituals (calendar invites, recurring 1:1 agenda items) are the mechanism that makes the plan live.

antipattern.description: Letting agents classify stakeholder levels from email tone alone.
antipattern.reason: Polite-tone bias causes systematic over-rating. Require behavioural evidence: attendance, decisions, public statements, response latency.
